entries.name: helix
entries.signature:
helix(
  revolutions: number(_),
  angleStart: number(Angle),
  ccw?: bool,
  radius?: number(Length),
  axis?: Axis3d | Edge,
  length?: number(Length),
  cylinder?: Solid,
): Helix
entries.description: Create a helix.

parameters.name: revolutions
parameters.description: Number of revolutions.

parameters.name: angleStart
parameters.description: Start angle.

parameters.name: ccw
parameters.description: Is the helix rotation counter clockwise? The default is `false`.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: Radius of the helix.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: Axis to use for the helix. The center of the helix's base will be at this axis's origin point.

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: Length of the helix. This is not necessary if the helix is created around an edge. If not given the length of the edge is used.

parameters.name: cylinder
parameters.description: Cylinder to create the helix on.

entries.name: offsetPlane
entries.description: Offset a plane by a distance along its normal.

parameters.name: plane
parameters.description: The plane (e.g. `XY`) which this new plane is created from.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Distance from the standard plane this new plane will be created at.

entries.name: clone
entries.signature: clone(@geometry: Sketch | Solid | ImportedGeometry): Sketch | Solid | ImportedGeometry
entries.description: Clone a sketch or solid.

parameters.name: geometry
parameters.description: The sketch, solid, or imported geometry to be cloned.

entries.name: assertIs
entries.description: Asserts that a value is the boolean value true.

parameters.name: actual
parameters.description: Value to check. If this is the boolean value true, assert passes. Otherwise it fails..

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: If the value was false, the program will terminate with this error message

entries.name: assert
entries.signature:
assert(
  @actual: number,
  isGreaterThan?: number,
  isLessThan?: number,
  isGreaterThanOrEqual?: number,
  isLessThanOrEqual?: number,
  isEqualTo?: number,
  tolerance?: number,
  error?: string,
)
entries.description: Check a value meets some expected conditions at runtime. Program terminates with an error if conditions aren't met. If you provide multiple conditions, they will all be checked and all must be met.

parameters.name: actual
parameters.description: Value to check. If this is the boolean value true, assert passes. Otherwise it fails..

parameters.name: isGreaterThan
parameters.description: Comparison argument. If given, checks the `actual` value is greater than this.

parameters.name: isLessThan
parameters.description: Comparison argument. If given, checks the `actual` value is less than this.

parameters.name: isGreaterThanOrEqual
parameters.description: Comparison argument. If given, checks the `actual` value is greater than or equal to this.

parameters.name: isLessThanOrEqual
parameters.description: Comparison argument. If given, checks the `actual` value is less than or equal to this.

parameters.name: isEqualTo
parameters.description: Comparison argument. If given, checks the `actual` value is less than or equal to this.

parameters.name: tolerance
parameters.description: If `isEqualTo` is used, this is the tolerance to allow for the comparison. This tolerance is used because KCL's number system has some floating-point imprecision when used with very large decimal places.

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: If the value was false, the program will terminate with this error message

entries.name: gdt::datum
entries.signature:
gdt::datum(
  face: TaggedFace,
  name: string,
  framePosition?: Point2d,
  framePlane?: Plane,
  fontPointSize?: number(_),
  fontScale?: number(_),
): GdtAnnotation
entries.description: GD&T datum feature.

parameters.name: face
parameters.description: The face to be annotated.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the datum.

parameters.name: framePosition
parameters.description: The position of the feature control frame relative to the leader arrow. The default is `[100mm, 100mm]`.

parameters.name: framePlane
parameters.description: The plane in which to display the feature control frame. The default is `XY`. Other standard planes like `XZ` and `YZ` can also be used. The frame may be displayed in a plane parallel to the given plane.

parameters.name: fontPointSize
parameters.description: The font point size to use for the annotation text rendering. The default is `36`.

parameters.name: fontScale
parameters.description: Scale to use for the annotation text after rendering with the point size. The default is `1.0`. Must be greater than `0`.

entries.name: gdt::flatness
entries.signature:
gdt::flatness(
  faces: [TaggedFace; 1+],
  tolerance: number(Length),
  precision?: number(_),
  framePosition?: Point2d,
  framePlane?: Plane,
  fontPointSize?: number(_),
  fontScale?: number(_),
): [GdtAnnotation; 1+]
entries.description: GD&T annotation specifying how flat faces should be.

parameters.name: faces
parameters.description: The faces to be annotated.

parameters.name: tolerance
parameters.description: The amount of deviation from a perfect plane that is acceptable.

parameters.name: precision
parameters.description: The number of decimal places to display. The default is `3`. Must be greater than or equal to `0` and less than or equal to `9`.

parameters.name: framePosition
parameters.description: The position of the feature control frame relative to the leader arrow. The default is `[100mm, 100mm]`.

parameters.name: framePlane
parameters.description: The plane in which to display the feature control frame. The default is `XY`. Other standard planes like `XZ` and `YZ` can also be used. The frame may be displayed in a plane parallel to the given plane.

parameters.name: fontPointSize
parameters.description: The font point size to use for the annotation text rendering. The default is `36`.

parameters.name: fontScale
parameters.description: Scale to use for the annotation text after rendering with the point size. The default is `1.0`. Must be greater than `0`.

entries.name: units::toMillimeters
entries.description: Convert a number to millimeters from its current units.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: units::toCentimeters
entries.description: Convert a number to centimeters from its current units.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: units::toMeters
entries.description: Convert a number to meters from its current units.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: units::toInches
entries.description: Convert a number to inches from its current units.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: units::toFeet
entries.description: Convert a number to feet from its current units.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: units::toYards
entries.description: Converts a number to yards from its current units.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: units::toRadians
entries.description: Converts a number to radians from its current units.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: units::toDegrees
entries.description: Converts a number to degrees from its current units.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: map
entries.description: Apply a function to every element of a list.

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: Input array. The output array is this input array, but every element has had the function `f` run on it.

parameters.name: f
parameters.description: A function. The output array is just the input array, but `f` has been run on every item.

entries.name: reduce
entries.description: Take a starting value. Then, for each element of an array, calculate the next value, using the previous value and the element.

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: Each element of this array gets run through the function `f`, combined with the previous output from `f`, and then used for the next run.

parameters.name: initial
parameters.description: The first time `f` is run, it will be called with the first item of `array` and this initial starting value.

parameters.name: f
parameters.description: Run once per item in the input `array`. This function takes an item from the array, and the previous output from `f` (or `initial` on the very first run). The final time `f` is run, its output is returned as the final output from `reduce`.

entries.name: push
entries.description: Append an element to the end of an array.

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: The array which you're adding a new item to.

parameters.name: item
parameters.description: The new item to add to the array

entries.name: pop
entries.description: Remove the last element from an array.

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: The array to pop from. Must not be empty.

entries.name: concat
entries.description: Combine two arrays into one by concatenating them.

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: The array of starting elements.

parameters.name: items
parameters.description: The array of ending elements.

entries.name: count
entries.description: Find the number of elements in an array.

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: The array whose length will be returned.

entries.name: cos
entries.description: Compute the cosine of a number.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: sin
entries.description: Compute the sine of a number.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: tan
entries.description: Compute the tangent of a number.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: acos
entries.description: Compute the arccosine of a number.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: asin
entries.description: Compute the arcsine of a number.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: atan
entries.description: Compute the arctangent of a number.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: atan2
entries.signature:
atan2(
  y: number(Length),
  x: number(Length),
): number(rad)
entries.description: Compute the four quadrant arctangent of Y and X.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: A number.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: polar
entries.signature:
polar(
  angle: number(rad),
  length: number(Length),
): Point2d
entries.description: Convert polar/sphere (azimuth, elevation, distance) coordinates to cartesian (x/y/z grid) coordinates.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: A number.

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: rem
entries.description: Compute the remainder after dividing `num` by `div`. If `num` is negative, the result will be too.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: The number which will be divided by `divisor`.

parameters.name: divisor
parameters.description: The number which will divide `num`.

entries.name: sqrt
entries.description: Compute the square root of a number.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: abs
entries.description: Compute the absolute value of a number.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: round
entries.description: Round a number to the nearest integer.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: floor
entries.description: Compute the largest integer less than or equal to a number.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: ceil
entries.description: Compute the smallest integer greater than or equal to a number.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: min
entries.description: Compute the minimum of the given arguments.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: An array of numbers to compute the minimum of.

entries.name: max
entries.description: Compute the maximum of the given arguments.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: An array of numbers to compute the maximum of.

entries.name: pow
entries.description: Compute the number to a power.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The number to raise.

parameters.name: exp
parameters.description: The power to raise to.

entries.name: log
entries.description: Compute the logarithm of the number with respect to an arbitrary base.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The number to compute the logarithm of.

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: The base of the logarithm.

entries.name: log2
entries.description: Compute the base 2 logarithm of the number.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: log10
entries.description: Compute the base 10 logarithm of the number.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: ln
entries.description: Compute the natural logarithm of the number.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: legLen
entries.signature:
legLen(
  hypotenuse: number(Length),
  leg: number(Length),
): number(Length)
entries.description: Compute the length of the given leg.

parameters.name: hypotenuse
parameters.description: The length of the triangle's hypotenuse.

parameters.name: leg
parameters.description: The length of one of the triangle's legs (i.e. non-hypotenuse side).

entries.name: legAngX
entries.signature:
legAngX(
  hypotenuse: number(Length),
  leg: number(Length),
): number(deg)
entries.description: Compute the angle of the given leg for x.

parameters.name: hypotenuse
parameters.description: The length of the triangle's hypotenuse.

parameters.name: leg
parameters.description: The length of one of the triangle's legs (i.e. non-hypotenuse side).

entries.name: legAngY
entries.signature:
legAngY(
  hypotenuse: number(Length),
  leg: number(Length),
): number(deg)
entries.description: Compute the angle of the given leg for y.

parameters.name: hypotenuse
parameters.description: The length of the triangle's hypotenuse.

parameters.name: leg
parameters.description: The length of one of the triangle's legs (i.e. non-hypotenuse side).

entries.name: startSketchOn
entries.signature:
startSketchOn(
  @planeOrSolid: Solid | Plane,
  face?: TaggedFace,
  normalToFace?: TaggedFace,
  alignAxis?: Axis2d,
  normalOffset?: number(Length),
): Plane | Face
entries.description: Start a new 2-dimensional sketch on a specific plane or face.

parameters.name: planeOrSolid
parameters.description: Profile whose start is being used.

parameters.name: face
parameters.description: Identify a face of a solid if a solid is specified as the input argument (`planeOrSolid`). Incompatible with `normalToFace`.

parameters.name: normalToFace
parameters.description: Identify a face of a solid if a solid is specified as the input argument. Starts a sketch on the plane orthogonal to this specified face. Incompatible with `face`, requires `alignAxis`.

parameters.name: alignAxis
parameters.description: If sketching normal to face, this axis will be the new local x axis of the sketch plane. The selected face's normal will be the local y axis. Incompatible with `face`, requires `normalToFace`.

parameters.name: normalOffset
parameters.description: Offset the sketch plane along its normal by the given amount. Incompatible with `face`, requires `normalToFace`.

entries.name: startProfile
entries.description: Start a new profile at a given point.

parameters.name: startProfileOn
parameters.description: What to start the profile on.

parameters.name: at
parameters.description: Where to start the profile. An absolute point.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Tag this first starting point.

entries.name: rectangle
entries.signature:
rectangle(
  @sketchOrSurface: Sketch | Plane | Face,
  width: number(Length),
  height: number(Length),
  center?: Point2d,
  corner?: Point2d,
): Sketch
entries.description: Sketch a rectangle.

parameters.name: sketchOrSurface
parameters.description: Sketch to extend, or plane or surface to sketch on.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Rectangle's width along X axis.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Rectangle's height along Y axis.

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The center of the rectangle. Either `corner` or `center` is required, but not both.

parameters.name: corner
parameters.description: The corner of the rectangle. Either `corner` or `center` is required, but not both. This will be the corner which is most negative on both X and Y axes.

entries.name: circle
entries.signature:
circle(
  @sketchOrSurface: Sketch | Plane | Face,
  center?: Point2d,
  radius?: number(Length),
  diameter?: number(Length),
  tag?: TagDecl,
): Sketch
entries.description: Construct a 2-dimensional circle, of the specified radius, centered at the provided (x, y) origin point.

parameters.name: sketchOrSurface
parameters.description: Sketch to extend, or plane or surface to sketch on.

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The center of the circle. If not given, defaults to `[0, 0]`.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The radius of the circle. Incompatible with `diameter`.

parameters.name: diameter
parameters.description: The diameter of the circle. Incompatible with `radius`.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this circle.

entries.name: ellipse
entries.signature:
ellipse(
  @sketchOrSurface: Sketch | Plane | Face,
  center: Point2d,
  minorRadius: number(Length),
  majorRadius?: number(Length),
  majorAxis?: Point2d,
  tag?: TagDecl,
): Sketch
entries.description: Construct a 2-dimensional ellipse, of the specified major/minor radius, centered at the provided (x, y) point.

parameters.name: sketchOrSurface
parameters.description: Sketch to extend, or plane or surface to sketch on.

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The center of the ellipse.

parameters.name: minorRadius
parameters.description: The minor radius of the ellipse.

parameters.name: majorRadius
parameters.description: The major radius of the ellipse. Equivalent to majorAxis = [majorRadius, 0].

parameters.name: majorAxis
parameters.description: The major axis of the ellipse.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this ellipse.

entries.name: extrude
entries.signature:
extrude(
  @sketches: [Sketch; 1+],
  length?: number(Length),
  to?: Point3d | Axis3d | Plane | Edge | Face | Sketch | Solid | TaggedEdge | TaggedFace,
  symmetric?: bool,
  bidirectionalLength?: number(Length),
  tagStart?: TagDecl,
  tagEnd?: TagDecl,
  twistAngle?: number(Angle),
  twistAngleStep?: number(Angle),
  twistCenter?: Point2d,
  method?: string,
): [Solid; 1+]
entries.description: Extend a 2-dimensional sketch through a third dimension in order to create new 3-dimensional volume, or if extruded into an existing volume, cut into an existing solid.

parameters.name: sketches
parameters.description: Which sketch or sketches should be extruded.

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: How far to extrude the given sketches. Incompatible with `to`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.type: Point3d | Axis3d | Plane | Edge | Face | Sketch | Solid | TaggedEdge | TaggedFace
parameters.description: Reference to extrude to. Incompatible with `length` and `twistAngle`.

parameters.name: symmetric
parameters.description: If true, the extrusion will happen symmetrically around the sketch. Otherwise, the extrusion will happen on only one side of the sketch.

parameters.name: bidirectionalLength
parameters.description: If specified, will also extrude in the opposite direction to 'distance' to the specified distance. If 'symmetric' is true, this value is ignored.

parameters.name: tagStart
parameters.description: A named tag for the face at the start of the extrusion, i.e. the original sketch.

parameters.name: tagEnd
parameters.description: A named tag for the face at the end of the extrusion, i.e. the new face created by extruding the original sketch.

parameters.name: twistAngle
parameters.description: If given, the sketch will be twisted around this angle while being extruded. Incompatible with `to`.

parameters.name: twistAngleStep
parameters.description: The size of each intermediate angle as the sketch twists around. Must be between 4 and 90 degrees. Only used if `twistAngle` is given, defaults to 15 degrees.

parameters.name: twistCenter
parameters.description: The center around which the sketch will be twisted. Relative to the sketch's center. Only used if `twistAngle` is given, defaults to [0, 0] i.e. sketch's center.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: The method used during extrusion, either `NEW` or `MERGE`. `NEW` creates a new object. `MERGE` merges the extruded objects together. The default is `MERGE`.

entries.name: revolve
entries.signature:
revolve(
  @sketches: [Sketch; 1+],
  axis: Axis2d | Edge,
  angle?: number(Angle),
  tolerance?: number(Length),
  symmetric?: bool,
  bidirectionalAngle?: number(Angle),
  tagStart?: TagDecl,
  tagEnd?: TagDecl,
): [Solid; 1+]
entries.description: Rotate a sketch around some provided axis, creating a solid from its extent.

parameters.name: sketches
parameters.description: The sketch or set of sketches that should be revolved

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: Axis of revolution.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: Angle to revolve (in degrees). Default is 360.

parameters.name: tolerance
parameters.description: Defines the smallest distance below which two entities are considered coincident, intersecting, coplanar, or similar. For most use cases, it should not be changed from its default value of 10^-7 millimeters.

parameters.name: symmetric
parameters.description: If true, the extrusion will happen symmetrically around the sketch. Otherwise, the extrusion will happen on only one side of the sketch.

parameters.name: bidirectionalAngle
parameters.description: If specified, will also revolve in the opposite direction to 'angle' to the specified angle. If 'symmetric' is true, this value is ignored.

parameters.name: tagStart
parameters.description: A named tag for the face at the start of the revolve, i.e. the original sketch.

parameters.name: tagEnd
parameters.description: A named tag for the face at the end of the revolve.

entries.name: patternTransform2d
entries.description: Just like `patternTransform`, but works on 2D sketches not 3D solids.

parameters.name: sketches
parameters.description: The sketch(es) to duplicate.

parameters.name: instances
parameters.description: The number of total instances. Must be greater than or equal to 1. This includes the original entity. For example, if instances is 2, there will be two copies -- the original, and one new copy. If instances is 1, this has no effect.

parameters.name: transform
parameters.description: How each replica should be transformed. The transform function takes a single parameter: an integer representing which number replication the transform is for. E.g. the first replica to be transformed will be passed the argument `1`. This simplifies your math: the transform function can rely on id `0` being the original instance passed into the `patternTransform`. See the examples.

parameters.name: useOriginal
parameters.description: If the target was sketched on an extrusion, setting this will use the original sketch as the target, not the entire joined solid.

entries.name: getOppositeEdge
entries.description: Get the opposite edge to the edge given.

parameters.name: edge
parameters.description: The tag of the edge you want to find the opposite edge of.

entries.name: getNextAdjacentEdge
entries.description: Get the next adjacent edge to the edge given.

parameters.name: edge
parameters.description: The tag of the edge you want to find the next adjacent edge of.

entries.name: getPreviousAdjacentEdge
entries.description: Get the previous adjacent edge to the edge given.

parameters.name: edge
parameters.description: The tag of the edge you want to find the previous adjacent edge of.

entries.name: getCommonEdge
entries.description: Get the shared edge between two faces.

parameters.name: faces
parameters.description: The tags of the faces you want to find the common edge between.

entries.name: circleThreePoint
entries.signature:
circleThreePoint(
  @sketchOrSurface: Sketch | Plane | Face,
  p1: Point2d,
  p2: Point2d,
  p3: Point2d,
  tag?: TagDecl,
): Sketch
entries.description: Construct a circle derived from 3 points.

parameters.name: sketchOrSurface
parameters.description: Plane or surface to sketch on.

parameters.name: p1
parameters.description: 1st point to derive the circle.

parameters.name: p2
parameters.description: 2nd point to derive the circle.

parameters.name: p3
parameters.description: 3rd point to derive the circle.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Identifier for the circle to reference elsewhere.

entries.name: polygon
entries.signature:
polygon(
  @sketchOrSurface: Sketch | Plane | Face,
  radius: number(Length),
  numSides: number(_),
  center: Point2d,
  inscribed?: bool,
): Sketch
entries.description: Create a regular polygon with the specified number of sides that is either inscribed or circumscribed around a circle of the specified radius.

parameters.name: sketchOrSurface
parameters.description: Plane or surface to sketch on.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The radius of the polygon.

parameters.name: numSides
parameters.description: The number of sides in the polygon.

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The center point of the polygon.

parameters.name: inscribed
parameters.description: Whether the polygon is inscribed (true, the default) or circumscribed (false) about a circle with the specified radius.

entries.name: sweep
entries.signature:
sweep(
  @sketches: [Sketch; 1+],
  path: Sketch | Helix,
  sectional?: bool,
  tolerance?: number(Length),
  relativeTo?: string,
  tagStart?: TagDecl,
  tagEnd?: TagDecl,
): [Solid; 1+]
entries.description: Extrude a sketch along a path.

parameters.name: sketches
parameters.description: The sketch or set of sketches that should be swept in space.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The path to sweep the sketch along.

parameters.name: sectional
parameters.description: If true, the sweep will be broken up into sub-sweeps (extrusions, revolves, sweeps) based on the trajectory path components.

parameters.name: tolerance
parameters.description: Defines the smallest distance below which two entities are considered coincident, intersecting, coplanar, or similar. For most use cases, it should not be changed from its default value of 10^-7 millimeters.

parameters.name: relativeTo
parameters.description: What is the sweep relative to? Can be either 'sketchPlane' or 'trajectoryCurve'.

parameters.name: tagStart
parameters.description: A named tag for the face at the start of the sweep, i.e. the original sketch.

parameters.name: tagEnd
parameters.description: A named tag for the face at the end of the sweep.

entries.name: loft
entries.signature:
loft(
  @sketches: [Sketch; 2+],
  vDegree?: number(_),
  bezApproximateRational?: bool,
  baseCurveIndex?: number(_),
  tolerance?: number(Length),
  tagStart?: TagDecl,
  tagEnd?: TagDecl,
): Solid
entries.description: Create a 3D surface or solid by interpolating between two or more sketches.

parameters.name: sketches
parameters.description: Which sketches to loft. Must include at least 2 sketches.

parameters.name: vDegree
parameters.description: Degree of the interpolation. Must be greater than zero. For example, use 2 for quadratic, or 3 for cubic interpolation in the V direction.

parameters.name: bezApproximateRational
parameters.description: Attempt to approximate rational curves (such as arcs) using a bezier. This will remove banding around interpolations between arcs and non-arcs. It may produce errors in other scenarios. Over time, this field won't be necessary.

parameters.name: baseCurveIndex
parameters.description: This can be set to override the automatically determined topological base curve, which is usually the first section encountered.

parameters.name: tolerance
parameters.description: Defines the smallest distance below which two entities are considered coincident, intersecting, coplanar, or similar. For most use cases, it should not be changed from its default value of 10^-7 millimeters.

parameters.name: tagStart
parameters.description: A named tag for the face at the start of the loft, i.e. the original sketch.

parameters.name: tagEnd
parameters.description: A named tag for the face at the end of the loft.

entries.name: patternLinear2d
entries.signature:
patternLinear2d(
  @sketches: [Sketch; 1+],
  instances: number(_),
  distance: number(Length),
  axis: Axis2d | Point2d,
  useOriginal?: bool,
): [Sketch; 1+]
entries.description: Repeat a 2-dimensional sketch along some dimension, with a dynamic amount of distance between each repetition, some specified number of times.

parameters.name: sketches
parameters.description: The sketch(es) to duplicate.

parameters.name: instances
parameters.description: The number of total instances. Must be greater than or equal to 1. This includes the original entity. For example, if instances is 2, there will be two copies -- the original, and one new copy. If instances is 1, this has no effect.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: Distance between each repetition. Also known as 'spacing'.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The axis of the pattern. A 2D vector.

parameters.name: useOriginal
parameters.description: If the target was sketched on an extrusion, setting this will use the original sketch as the target, not the entire joined solid.

entries.name: patternCircular2d
entries.signature:
patternCircular2d(
  @sketches: [Sketch; 1+],
  instances: number(_),
  center: Point2d,
  arcDegrees?: number(Angle),
  rotateDuplicates?: bool,
  useOriginal?: bool,
): [Sketch; 1+]
entries.description: Repeat a 2-dimensional sketch some number of times along a partial or complete circle some specified number of times. Each object may additionally be rotated along the circle, ensuring orientation of the solid with respect to the center of the circle is maintained.

parameters.name: sketches
parameters.description: The sketch(es) to duplicate.

parameters.name: instances
parameters.description: The number of total instances. Must be greater than or equal to 1. This includes the original entity. For example, if instances is 2, there will be two copies -- the original, and one new copy. If instances is 1, this has no effect.

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The center about which to make the pattern. This is a 2D vector.

parameters.name: arcDegrees
parameters.description: The arc angle (in degrees) to place the repetitions. Must be greater than 0.

parameters.name: rotateDuplicates
parameters.description: Whether or not to rotate the duplicates as they are copied.

parameters.name: useOriginal
parameters.description: If the target was sketched on an extrusion, setting this will use the original sketch as the target, not the entire joined solid.

entries.name: segEnd
entries.description: Compute the ending point of the provided line segment.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The line segment being queried by its tag.

entries.name: segEndX
entries.description: Compute the ending point of the provided line segment along the 'x' axis.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The line segment being queried by its tag.

entries.name: segEndY
entries.description: Compute the ending point of the provided line segment along the 'y' axis.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The line segment being queried by its tag.

entries.name: segStart
entries.description: Compute the starting point of the provided line segment.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The line segment being queried by its tag.

entries.name: segStartX
entries.description: Compute the starting point of the provided line segment along the 'x' axis.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The line segment being queried by its tag.

entries.name: segStartY
entries.description: Compute the starting point of the provided line segment along the 'y' axis.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The line segment being queried by its tag.

entries.name: lastSegX
entries.description: Extract the 'x' axis value of the last line segment in the provided 2-d sketch.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: The sketch whose line segment is being queried.

entries.name: lastSegY
entries.description: Extract the 'y' axis value of the last line segment in the provided 2-d sketch.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: The sketch whose line segment is being queried.

entries.name: segLen
entries.description: Compute the length of the provided line segment.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The line segment being queried by its tag.

entries.name: segAng
entries.description: Compute the angle (in degrees) of the provided line segment.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The line segment being queried by its tag.

entries.name: tangentToEnd
entries.description: Returns the angle coming out of the end of the segment in degrees.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The line segment being queried by its tag.

entries.name: profileStart
entries.description: Extract the provided 2-dimensional sketch's profile's origin value.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: Profile whose start is being used.

entries.name: profileStartX
entries.description: Extract the provided 2-dimensional sketch's profile's origin's 'x' value.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: Profile whose start is being used.

entries.name: profileStartY
entries.description: Extract the provided 2-dimensional sketch's profile's origin's 'y' value.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: Profile whose start is being used.

entries.name: involuteCircular
entries.signature:
involuteCircular(
  @sketch: Sketch,
  angle: number(Angle),
  startRadius?: number(Length),
  endRadius?: number(Length),
  startDiameter?: number(Length),
  endDiameter?: number(Length),
  reverse?: bool,
  tag?: TagDecl,
): Sketch
entries.description: Extend the current sketch with a new involute circular curve.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: The angle to rotate the involute by. A value of zero will produce a curve with a tangent along the x-axis at the start point of the curve.

parameters.name: startRadius
parameters.description: The involute is described between two circles, startRadius is the radius of the inner circle. Either `startRadius` or `startDiameter` must be given (but not both).

parameters.name: endRadius
parameters.description: The involute is described between two circles, endRadius is the radius of the outer circle. Either `endRadius` or `endDiameter` must be given (but not both).

parameters.name: startDiameter
parameters.description: The involute is described between two circles, startDiameter describes the inner circle. Either `startRadius` or `startDiameter` must be given (but not both).

parameters.name: endDiameter
parameters.description: The involute is described between two circles, endDiameter describes the outer circle. Either `endRadius` or `endDiameter` must be given (but not both).

parameters.name: reverse
parameters.description: If reverse is true, the segment will start from the end of the involute, otherwise it will start from that start.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this line.

entries.name: line
entries.description: Extend the current sketch with a new straight line.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: endAbsolute
parameters.description: Which absolute point should this line go to? Incompatible with `end`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: How far away (along the X and Y axes) should this line go? Incompatible with `endAbsolute`.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this line.

entries.name: xLine
entries.description: Draw a line relative to the current origin to a specified distance away from the current position along the 'x' axis.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: How far away along the X axis should this line go? Incompatible with `endAbsolute`.

parameters.name: endAbsolute
parameters.description: Which absolute X value should this line go to? Incompatible with `length`.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this line.

entries.name: yLine
entries.description: Draw a line relative to the current origin to a specified distance away from the current position along the 'y' axis.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: How far away along the Y axis should this line go? Incompatible with `endAbsolute`.

parameters.name: endAbsolute
parameters.description: Which absolute Y value should this line go to? Incompatible with `length`.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this line.

entries.name: angledLine
entries.signature:
angledLine(
  @sketch: Sketch,
  angle: number(Angle),
  length?: number(Length),
  lengthX?: number(Length),
  lengthY?: number(Length),
  endAbsoluteX?: number(Length),
  endAbsoluteY?: number(Length),
  tag?: TagDecl,
): Sketch
entries.description: Draw a line segment relative to the current origin using the polar measure of some angle and distance.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: Which angle should the line be drawn at?

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: Draw the line this distance along the given angle. Only one of `length`, `lengthX`, `lengthY`, `endAbsoluteX`, `endAbsoluteY` can be given.

parameters.name: lengthX
parameters.description: Draw the line this distance along the X axis. Only one of `length`, `lengthX`, `lengthY`, `endAbsoluteX`, `endAbsoluteY` can be given.

parameters.name: lengthY
parameters.description: Draw the line this distance along the Y axis. Only one of `length`, `lengthX`, `lengthY`, `endAbsoluteX`, `endAbsoluteY` can be given.

parameters.name: endAbsoluteX
parameters.description: Draw the line along the given angle until it reaches this point along the X axis. Only one of `length`, `lengthX`, `lengthY`, `endAbsoluteX`, `endAbsoluteY` can be given.

parameters.name: endAbsoluteY
parameters.description: Draw the line along the given angle until it reaches this point along the Y axis. Only one of `length`, `lengthX`, `lengthY`, `endAbsoluteX`, `endAbsoluteY` can be given.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this line.

entries.name: angledLineThatIntersects
entries.signature:
angledLineThatIntersects(
  @sketch: Sketch,
  angle: number(Angle),
  intersectTag: TaggedEdge,
  offset?: number(Length),
  tag?: TagDecl,
): Sketch
entries.description: Draw an angled line from the current origin, constructing a line segment such that the newly created line intersects the desired target line segment.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: Which angle should the line be drawn at?

parameters.name: intersectTag
parameters.description: The tag of the line to intersect with.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The offset from the intersecting line.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this line.

entries.name: close
entries.description: Construct a line segment from the current origin back to the profile's origin, ensuring the resulting 2-dimensional sketch is not open-ended.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: The sketch you want to close.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this line.

entries.name: arc
entries.signature:
arc(
  @sketch: Sketch,
  angleStart?: number(Angle),
  angleEnd?: number(Angle),
  radius?: number(Length),
  diameter?: number(Length),
  interiorAbsolute?: Point2d,
  endAbsolute?: Point2d,
  tag?: TagDecl,
): Sketch
entries.description: Draw a curved line segment along an imaginary circle.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: angleStart
parameters.description: Where along the circle should this arc start?

parameters.name: angleEnd
parameters.description: Where along the circle should this arc end?

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: How large should the circle be? Incompatible with `diameter`.

parameters.name: diameter
parameters.description: How large should the circle be? Incompatible with `radius`.

parameters.name: interiorAbsolute
parameters.description: Any point between the arc's start and end? Requires `endAbsolute`. Incompatible with `angleStart` or `angleEnd`.

parameters.name: endAbsolute
parameters.description: Where should this arc end? Requires `interiorAbsolute`. Incompatible with `angleStart` or `angleEnd`.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this arc.

entries.name: tangentialArc
entries.description: Starting at the current sketch's origin, draw a curved line segment along some part of an imaginary circle until it reaches the desired (x, y) coordinates.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: endAbsolute
parameters.description: Which absolute point should this arc go to? Incompatible with `end`, `radius`, and `offset`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: How far away (along the X and Y axes) should this arc go? Incompatible with `endAbsolute`, `radius`, and `offset`.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: Radius of the imaginary circle. `angle` must be given. Incompatible with `end` and `endAbsolute` and `diameter`.

parameters.name: diameter
parameters.description: Diameter of the imaginary circle. `angle` must be given. Incompatible with `end` and `endAbsolute` and `radius`.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: Offset of the arc. `radius` must be given. Incompatible with `end` and `endAbsolute`.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this arc.

entries.name: bezierCurve
entries.description: Draw a smooth, continuous, curved line segment from the current origin to the desired (x, y), using a number of control points to shape the curve's shape.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: control1
parameters.description: First control point for the cubic.

parameters.name: control2
parameters.description: Second control point for the cubic.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: How far away (along the X and Y axes) should this line go?

parameters.name: control1Absolute
parameters.description: First control point for the cubic. Absolute point.

parameters.name: control2Absolute
parameters.description: Second control point for the cubic. Absolute point.

parameters.name: endAbsolute
parameters.description: Coordinate on the plane at which this line should end.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this line.

entries.name: subtract2d
entries.description: Use a 2-dimensional sketch to cut a hole in another 2-dimensional sketch.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: tool
parameters.description: The shape(s) which should be cut out of the sketch.

entries.name: conic
entries.signature:
conic(
  @sketch: Sketch,
  interiorAbsolute?: Point2d,
  endAbsolute?: Point2d,
  interior?: Point2d,
  end?: Point2d,
  coefficients?: [number; 6],
  startTangent?: Point2d,
  endTangent?: Point2d,
  tag?: TagDecl,
): Sketch
entries.description: Add a conic section to an existing sketch.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: interiorAbsolute
parameters.description: Any point between the segment's start and end. Requires `endAbsolute`. Incompatible with `interior` or `end`.

parameters.name: endAbsolute
parameters.description: Where should this segment end? Requires `interiorAbsolute`. Incompatible with `interior` or `end`.

parameters.name: interior
parameters.description: Any point between the segment's start and end. This point is relative to the start point. Requires `end`. Incompatible with `interiorAbsolute` or `endAbsolute`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Where should this segment end? This point is relative to the start point. Requires `interior`. Incompatible with `interiorAbsolute` or `endAbsolute`.

parameters.name: coefficients
parameters.description: The coefficients [a, b, c, d, e, f] of the generic conic equation ax^2 + by^2 + cxy + dx + ey + f = 0. If provided the start and end tangents will be calculated using this equation. Incompatible with `startTangent` and `endTangent`.

parameters.name: startTangent
parameters.description: The tangent of the conic section at the start. If not provided the tangent of the previous path segment is used. Incompatible with `coefficients`.

parameters.name: endTangent
parameters.description: The tangent of the conic section at the end. Incompatible with `coefficients`.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this segment.

entries.name: parabolic
entries.description: Add a parabolic segment to an existing sketch.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Where should the path end? Relative to the start point. Incompatible with `interiorAbsolute` or `endAbsolute`.

parameters.name: endAbsolute
parameters.description: Where should this segment end? Requires `interiorAbsolute`. Incompatible with `interior` or `end`.

parameters.name: coefficients
parameters.description: The coefficients [a, b, c] of the parabolic equation y = ax^2 + bx + c. Incompatible with `interior`.

parameters.name: interior
parameters.description: A point between the segment's start and end that lies on the parabola. Incompatible with `coefficients` or `interiorAbsolute` or `endAbsolute`.

parameters.name: interiorAbsolute
parameters.description: Any point between the segment's start and end. Requires `endAbsolute`. Incompatible with `coefficients` or `interior` or `end`.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this segment.

entries.name: parabolicPoint
entries.signature:
parabolicPoint(
  coefficients: [number; 3],
  x?: number(Length),
  y?: number(Length),
): Point2d
entries.description: Calculate the point (x, y) on a parabola given x or y and the coefficients [a, b, c] of the parabola.

parameters.name: coefficients
parameters.description: The coefficients [a, b, c] of the parabolic equation y = ax^2 + bx + c.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The x value. Calculates y and returns (x, y). Incompatible with `y`.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: The y value. Calculates x and returns (x, y). Incompatible with `x`.

entries.name: hyperbolic
entries.description: Add a hyperbolic section to an existing sketch.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: semiMajor
parameters.description: The semi major value, a, of the hyperbolic equation x^2 / a ^ 2 - y^2 / b^2 = 1.

parameters.name: semiMinor
parameters.description: The semi minor value, b, of the hyperbolic equation x^2 / a ^ 2 - y^2 / b^2 = 1.

parameters.name: interiorAbsolute
parameters.description: Any point between the segment's start and end. Requires `endAbsolute`. Incompatible with `interior` or `end`.

parameters.name: endAbsolute
parameters.description: Where should this segment end? Requires `interiorAbsolute`. Incompatible with `interior` or `end`.

parameters.name: interior
parameters.description: Any point between the segment's start and end. This point is relative to the start point. Requires `end`. Incompatible with `interiorAbsolute` or `endAbsolute`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Where should this segment end? This point is relative to the start point. Requires `interior`. Incompatible with `interiorAbsolute` or `endAbsolute`.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this arc.

entries.name: hyperbolicPoint
entries.signature:
hyperbolicPoint(
  semiMajor: number,
  semiMinor: number,
  x?: number(Length),
  y?: number(Length),
): Point2d
entries.description: Calculate the point (x, y) on a hyperbola given x or y and the semi major/minor values of the hyperbolic.

parameters.name: semiMajor
parameters.description: The semi major value, a, of the hyperbolic equation x^2 / a ^ 2 - y^2 / b^2 = 1.

parameters.name: semiMinor
parameters.description: The semi minor value, b, of the hyperbolic equation x^2 / a ^ 2 - y^2 / b^2 = 1.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The x value. Calculates y and returns (x, y). Incompatible with `y`.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: The y value. Calculates x and returns (x, y). Incompatible with `x`.

entries.name: elliptic
entries.signature:
elliptic(
  @sketch: Sketch,
  center: Point2d,
  angleStart: number(Angle),
  angleEnd: number(Angle),
  minorRadius: number(Length),
  majorRadius?: number(Length),
  majorAxis?: Point2d,
  tag?: TagDecl,
): Sketch
entries.description: Add an elliptic section to an existing sketch.

parameters.name: sketch
parameters.description: Which sketch should this path be added to?

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The center of the ellipse.

parameters.name: angleStart
parameters.description: Where along the ellptic should this segment start?

parameters.name: angleEnd
parameters.description: Where along the ellptic should this segment end?

parameters.name: minorRadius
parameters.description: The minor radius, b, of the elliptic equation x^2 / a^2 + y^2 / b^2 = 1.

parameters.name: majorRadius
parameters.description: The major radius, a, of the elliptic equation x^2 / a^2 + y^2 / b^2 = 1. Equivalent to majorAxis = [majorRadius, 0].

parameters.name: majorAxis
parameters.description: The major axis of the elliptic.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this arc.

entries.name: ellipticPoint
entries.signature:
ellipticPoint(
  majorRadius: number,
  minorRadius: number,
  x?: number(Length),
  y?: number(Length),
): Point2d
entries.description: Calculate the point (x, y) on an ellipse given x or y and the center and major/minor radii of the ellipse.

parameters.name: majorRadius
parameters.description: The major radius, a, of the elliptic equation x^2 / a ^ 2 + y^2 / b^2 = 1.

parameters.name: minorRadius
parameters.description: The minor radius, b, of the hyperbolic equation x^2 / a ^ 2 + y^2 / b^2 = 1.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The x value. Calculates y and returns (x, y). Incompatible with `y`.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: The y value. Calculates x and returns (x, y). Incompatible with `x`.

entries.name: planeOf
entries.description: Find the plane a face lies on. Returns an error if the face doesn't lie on any plane (for example, the curved face of a cylinder)

parameters.name: solid
parameters.description: The solid whose face is being queried.

parameters.name: face
parameters.description: Find the plane which this face lies on.

entries.name: fillet
entries.signature:
fillet(
  @solid: Solid,
  radius: number(Length),
  tags: [Edge; 1+],
  tolerance?: number(Length),
  tag?: TagDecl,
): Solid
entries.description: Blend a transitional edge along a tagged path, smoothing the sharp edge.

parameters.name: solid
parameters.description: The solid whose edges should be filletted

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The radius of the fillet

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: The paths you want to fillet

parameters.name: tolerance
parameters.description: Defines the smallest distance below which two entities are considered coincident, intersecting, coplanar, or similar. For most use cases, it should not be changed from its default value of 10^-7 millimeters.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this fillet

entries.name: chamfer
entries.signature:
chamfer(
  @solid: Solid,
  length: number(Length),
  tags: [Edge; 1+],
  secondLength?: number(Length),
  angle?: number(Angle),
  tag?: TagDecl,
): Solid
entries.description: Cut a straight transitional edge along a tagged path.

parameters.name: solid
parameters.description: The solid whose edges should be chamfered

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: Chamfering cuts away two faces to create a third face. This is the length to chamfer away from each face. The larger this length to chamfer away, the larger the new face will be.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: The paths you want to chamfer

parameters.name: secondLength
parameters.description: Chamfering cuts away two faces to create a third face. If this argument isn't given, the lengths chamfered away from both the first and second face are both given by `length`. If this argument _is_ given, it determines how much is cut away from the second face. Incompatible with `angle`.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: Chamfering cuts away two faces to create a third face. This argument determines the angle between the two cut edges. Requires `length`, incompatible with `secondLength`. The valid range is 0deg < angle < 90deg.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Create a new tag which refers to this chamfer

entries.name: shell
entries.description: Remove volume from a 3-dimensional shape such that a wall of the provided thickness remains, taking volume starting at the provided face, leaving it open in that direction.

parameters.name: solids
parameters.description: Which solid (or solids) to shell out

parameters.name: thickness
parameters.description: The thickness of the shell

parameters.name: faces
parameters.description: The faces you want removed

entries.name: hollow
entries.description: Make the inside of a 3D object hollow.

parameters.name: solid
parameters.description: Which solid to hollow out

parameters.name: thickness
parameters.description: The thickness of the remaining shell

entries.name: patternTransform
entries.description: Repeat a 3-dimensional solid, changing it each time.

parameters.name: solids
parameters.description: The solid(s) to duplicate.

parameters.name: instances
parameters.description: The number of total instances. Must be greater than or equal to 1. This includes the original entity. For example, if instances is 2, there will be two copies -- the original, and one new copy. If instances is 1, this has no effect.

parameters.name: transform
parameters.description: How each replica should be transformed. The transform function takes a single parameter: an integer representing which number replication the transform is for. E.g. the first replica to be transformed will be passed the argument `1`. This simplifies your math: the transform function can rely on id `0` being the original instance passed into the `patternTransform`. See the examples.

parameters.name: useOriginal
parameters.description: If the target was sketched on an extrusion, setting this will use the original sketch as the target, not the entire joined solid.

entries.name: patternLinear3d
entries.signature:
patternLinear3d(
  @solids: [Solid; 1+],
  instances: number(_),
  distance: number(Length),
  axis: Axis3d | Point3d,
  useOriginal?: bool,
): [Solid; 1+]
entries.description: Repeat a 3-dimensional solid along a linear path, with a dynamic amount of distance between each repetition, some specified number of times.

parameters.name: solids
parameters.description: The solid(s) to duplicate.

parameters.name: instances
parameters.description: The number of total instances. Must be greater than or equal to 1. This includes the original entity. For example, if instances is 2, there will be two copies -- the original, and one new copy. If instances is 1, this has no effect.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: Distance between each repetition. Also known as 'spacing'.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The axis of the pattern. A 3D vector.

parameters.name: useOriginal
parameters.description: If the target was sketched on an extrusion, setting this will use the original sketch as the target, not the entire joined solid.

entries.name: patternCircular3d
entries.signature:
patternCircular3d(
  @solids: [Solid; 1+],
  instances: number(_),
  axis: Axis3d | Point3d,
  center: Point3d,
  arcDegrees?: number(deg),
  rotateDuplicates?: bool,
  useOriginal?: bool,
): [Solid; 1+]
entries.description: Repeat a 3-dimensional solid some number of times along a partial or complete circle some specified number of times. Each object may additionally be rotated along the circle, ensuring orientation of the solid with respect to the center of the circle is maintained.

parameters.name: solids
parameters.description: The solid(s) to pattern.

parameters.name: instances
parameters.description: The number of total instances. Must be greater than or equal to 1. This includes the original entity. For example, if instances is 2, there will be two copies -- the original, and one new copy. If instances is 1, this has no effect.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The axis of the pattern. A 3D vector.

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The center about which to make the pattern. This is a 3D vector.

parameters.name: arcDegrees
parameters.description: "The arc angle to place the repetitions. Must be greater than 0.

parameters.name: rotateDuplicates
parameters.description: Whether or not to rotate the duplicates as they are copied.

parameters.name: useOriginal
parameters.description: If the target was sketched on an extrusion, setting this will use the original sketch as the target, not the entire joined solid.

entries.name: union
entries.description: Union two or more solids into a single solid.

parameters.name: solids
parameters.description: The solids to union.

parameters.name: tolerance
parameters.description: Defines the smallest distance below which two entities are considered coincident, intersecting, coplanar, or similar. For most use cases, it should not be changed from its default value of 10^-7 millimeters.

entries.name: intersect
entries.description: Intersect returns the shared volume between multiple solids, preserving only overlapping regions.

parameters.name: solids
parameters.description: The solids to intersect.

parameters.name: tolerance
parameters.description: Defines the smallest distance below which two entities are considered coincident, intersecting, coplanar, or similar. For most use cases, it should not be changed from its default value of 10^-7 millimeters.

entries.name: subtract
entries.description: Subtract removes tool solids from base solids, leaving the remaining material.

parameters.name: solids
parameters.description: The solids to use as the base to subtract from.

parameters.name: tools
parameters.description: The solids to subtract.

parameters.name: tolerance
parameters.description: Defines the smallest distance below which two entities are considered coincident, intersecting, coplanar, or similar. For most use cases, it should not be changed from its default value of 10^-7 millimeters.

entries.name: appearance
entries.description: Set the appearance of a solid. This only works on solids, not sketches or individual paths.

parameters.name: solids
parameters.description: The The solid(s) whose appearance is being set.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: Color of the new material, a hex string like '#ff0000'.

parameters.name: metalness
parameters.description: Metalness of the new material, a percentage like 95.7.

parameters.name: roughness
parameters.description: Roughness of the new material, a percentage like 95.7.

entries.name: mirror2d
entries.description: Mirror a sketch.

parameters.name: sketches
parameters.description: The sketch or sketches to be reflected.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The axis to reflect around.

entries.name: translate
entries.signature:
translate(
  @objects: [Solid; 1+] | [Sketch; 1+] | ImportedGeometry,
  x?: number(Length),
  y?: number(Length),
  z?: number(Length),
  global?: bool,
  xyz?: [number(Length); 3],
): [Solid; 1+] | [Sketch; 1+] | ImportedGeometry
entries.description: Move a solid or a sketch.

parameters.name: objects
parameters.description: The solid, sketch, or set of solids or sketches to move.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The amount to move the solid or sketch along the x axis.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: The amount to move the solid or sketch along the y axis.

parameters.name: z
parameters.description: The amount to move the solid or sketch along the z axis.

parameters.name: global
parameters.description: If true, the transform is applied in global space. The origin of the model will move. By default, the transform is applied in local sketch axis, therefore the origin will not move.

parameters.name: xyz
parameters.description: If given, interpret this point as 3 distances, along each of [X, Y, Z] and translate by each of them.

entries.name: rotate
entries.signature:
rotate(
  @objects: [Solid; 1+] | [Sketch; 1+] | ImportedGeometry,
  roll?: number(Angle),
  pitch?: number(Angle),
  yaw?: number(Angle),
  axis?: Axis3d | Point3d,
  angle?: number(Angle),
  global?: bool,
): [Solid; 1+] | [Sketch; 1+] | ImportedGeometry
entries.description: Rotate a solid or a sketch.

parameters.name: objects
parameters.description: The solid, sketch, or set of solids or sketches to rotate.

parameters.name: roll
parameters.description: The roll angle. Must be between -360deg and 360deg.

parameters.name: pitch
parameters.description: The pitch angle. Must be between -360deg and 360deg.

parameters.name: yaw
parameters.description: The yaw angle. Must be between -360deg and 360deg.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The axis to rotate around. Must be used with `angle`.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: The angle to rotate. Must be used with `axis`. Must be between -360deg and 360deg.

parameters.name: global
parameters.description: If true, the transform is applied in global space. The origin of the model will move. By default, the transform is applied in local sketch axis, therefore the origin will not move.

entries.name: scale
entries.signature:
scale(
  @objects: [Solid; 1+] | [Sketch; 1+] | ImportedGeometry,
  x?: number(_),
  y?: number(_),
  z?: number(_),
  global?: bool,
  factor?: number(_),
): [Solid; 1+] | [Sketch; 1+] | ImportedGeometry
entries.description: Scale a solid or a sketch.

parameters.name: objects
parameters.description: The solid, sketch, or set of solids or sketches to scale.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The scale factor for the x axis.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: The scale factor for the y axis.

parameters.name: z
parameters.description: The scale factor for the z axis.

parameters.name: global
parameters.description: If true, the transform is applied in global space. The origin of the model will move. By default, the transform is applied in local sketch axis, therefore the origin will not move.

parameters.name: factor
parameters.description: If given, scale the solid by this much. Equivalent to setting `x`, `y` and `z` all to this number. Incompatible with `x`, `y` or `z`.

entries.name: appearance::hexString
entries.description: Build a color from its red, green and blue components. These must be between 0 and 255.

parameters.name: rgb
parameters.description: The red, blue and green components of the color. Must be between 0 and 255.

entries.name: vector::add
entries.description: Adds every element of u to its corresponding element in v. Both vectors must have the same length. Returns a new vector of the same length. In other words, component-wise addition.

entries.name: vector::sub
entries.description: Subtracts from every element of u its corresponding element in v. Both vectors must have the same length. Returns a new vector of the same length. In other words, component-wise subtraction.

entries.name: vector::mul
entries.description: Multiplies every element of u by its corresponding element in v. Both vectors must have the same length. Returns a new vector of the same length. In other words, component-wise multiplication.

entries.name: vector::div
entries.description: Divides every element of u by its corresponding element in v. Both vectors must have the same length. Returns a new vector of the same length. In other words, component-wise division.

entries.name: vector::cross
entries.description: Find the cross product of two 3D points or vectors.

parameters.name: u
parameters.description: A point in three dimensional space.

parameters.name: v
parameters.description: A point in three dimensional space.

entries.name: vector::dot
entries.description: Find the dot product of two points or vectors of any dimension.

entries.name: vector::magnitude
entries.description: Find the Euclidean distance of a vector.

entries.name: vector::normalize
entries.description: Normalize a vector (with any number of dimensions)

entries.name: hole::simple
entries.description: A hole top with no decoration.

entries.name: hole::counterbore
entries.description: Cut a straight vertical counterbore at the top of the hole. Typically used when a fastener (e.g. the head cap on a screw) needs to sit flush with the solid's surface.

parameters.name: diameter
parameters.description: A number.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: hole::countersink
entries.description: Cut an angled countersink at the top of the hole. Typically used when a conical screw head has to sit flush with the surface being cut into.

parameters.name: diameter
parameters.description: A number.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: hole::blind
entries.description: The hole has the given blind depth.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description: A number.

parameters.name: diameter
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: hole::drill
entries.description: End the hole in an angle, like the end of a drill.

parameters.name: pointAngle
parameters.description: A number.

entries.name: hole::flat
entries.description: End the hole flat.

entries.name: hole::hole
entries.signature:
hole::hole(
  @solid: Solid,
  face: TaggedFace,
  holeBottom,
  holeBody,
  holeType,
  cutAt: [number(Length); 2],
)
entries.description: From the hole's parts (bottom, middle, top), cut the hole into the given solid, at the given 2D position on the given face.

parameters.name: solid
parameters.description: Which solid to add a hole to.

parameters.name: face
parameters.description: Which face of the solid to add the hole to. Controls the orientation of the hole.

parameters.name: holeBottom
parameters.description: Define bottom feature of the hole. E.g. drilled or flat.

parameters.name: holeBody
parameters.description: Define the main length of the hole. E.g. a blind distance.

parameters.name: holeType
parameters.description: Define the top feature of the hole. E.g. countersink, counterbore, simple.

parameters.name: cutAt
parameters.description: Where to place the cut on the given face of the solid. Given as absolute coordinates in the global scene.

entries.name: hole::holes
entries.signature:
hole::holes(
  @solid: Solid,
  face: TaggedFace,
  holeBottom,
  holeBody,
  holeType,
  cutsAt: [[number(Length); 2]],
)
entries.description: From the hole's parts (bottom, middle, top), cut the hole into the given solid, at each of the given 2D positions on the given face. Basically like function `hole` but it takes multiple 2D positions in `cutsAt`.

parameters.name: solid
parameters.description: Which solid to add a hole to.

parameters.name: face
parameters.description: Which face of the solid to add the hole to. Controls the orientation of the hole.

parameters.name: holeBottom
parameters.description: Define bottom feature of the hole. E.g. drilled or flat.

parameters.name: holeBody
parameters.description: Define the main length of the hole. E.g. a blind distance.

parameters.name: holeType
parameters.description: Define the top feature of the hole. E.g. countersink, counterbore, simple.

parameters.name: cutsAt
parameters.description: Where to place the holes, given as absolute coordinates in the global scene.

entries.name: hole::holesLinear
entries.signature:
hole::holesLinear(
  @solid: Solid,
  face: TaggedFace,
  holeBottom,
  holeBody,
  holeType,
  cutAt: [number(Length); 2],
  instances: number(_),
  distance,
  axis: Axis2d | Point2d,
)
entries.description: Place the given holes in a line. Basically like function `hole` but cuts multiple holes in a line. Works like linear patterns.

parameters.name: solid
parameters.description: Which solid to add a hole to.

parameters.name: face
parameters.description: Which face of the solid to add the hole to. Controls the orientation of the hole.

parameters.name: holeBottom
parameters.description: Define bottom feature of the hole. E.g. drilled or flat.

parameters.name: holeBody
parameters.description: Define the main length of the hole. E.g. a blind distance.

parameters.name: holeType
parameters.description: Define the top feature of the hole. E.g. countersink, counterbore, simple.

parameters.name: cutAt
parameters.description: Where to place the first cut in the linear pattern, given as absolute coordinates in the global scene.

parameters.name: instances
parameters.description: How many holes to cut.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: How far between each hole

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: Along which axis should the holes be cut?

entries.name: any
entries.description: The `any` type is the type of all possible values in KCL. I.e., if a function accepts an argument with type `any`, then it can accept any value.

entries.name: none
entries.description: The type of the none (aka null) value.

entries.name: number
entries.description: A number.

entries.name: bool
entries.description: A boolean value.

entries.name: string
entries.description: A sequence of characters

entries.name: TagDecl
entries.description: Tags are used to give a name (tag) to a specific path.

entries.name: TaggedEdge
entries.description: A tag which references a line, arc, or other edge in a sketch or an edge of a solid.

entries.name: TaggedFace
entries.description: A tag which references a face of a solid, including the distinguished tags `START` and `END`.

entries.name: ImportedGeometry
entries.description: Represents geometry which is defined using some other CAD system and imported into KCL.

entries.name: fn
entries.description: The type of any function in KCL.

entries.name: Plane
entries.description: An abstract plane.

entries.name: Sketch
entries.description: A sketch is a collection of paths.

entries.name: Solid
entries.description: A solid is a collection of extruded surfaces.

entries.name: Face
entries.description: A face of a solid.

entries.name: Helix
entries.description: A helix; created by the `helix` function.

entries.name: Edge
entries.description: An edge of a solid.

entries.name: Point2d
entries.description: A point in two dimensional space.

entries.name: Point3d
entries.description: A point in three dimensional space.

entries.name: Axis2d
entries.description: An abstract and infinite line in 2d space.

entries.name: Axis3d
entries.description: An abstract and infinite line in 3d space.

entries.name: GdtAnnotation
entries.description: A GD&T annotation.

entries.name: XY
entries.description: An abstract 3d plane aligned with the X and Y axes. Its normal is the positive Z axis.

entries.name: XZ
entries.description: An abstract 3d plane aligned with the X and Z axes. Its normal is the negative Y axis.

entries.name: YZ
entries.description: An abstract 3d plane aligned with the Y and Z axes. Its normal is the positive X axis.

entries.name: X
entries.description: The X-axis (can be used in both 2d and 3d contexts).

entries.name: Y
entries.description: The Y-axis (can be used in both 2d and 3d contexts).

entries.name: Z
entries.description: The 3D Z-axis.

entries.name: START
entries.description: Identifies the starting face of an extrusion. I.e., the face which is extruded.

entries.name: END
entries.description: Identifies the ending face of an extrusion. I.e., the new face created by an extrusion.

entries.name: NEW
entries.description: Specifies that a new object is created during extrusion.

entries.name: MERGE
entries.description: Specifies that the extrusion will be pulled into or pushed out of the existing object, modifying it without creating a new object.

entries.name: PI
entries.description: The value of `pi`, Archimedes’ constant (π).

entries.name: E
entries.description: The value of Euler’s number `e`.

entries.name: TAU
entries.description: The value of `tau`, the full circle constant (τ). Equal to 2π.

entries.name: turns::ZERO
entries.description: No turn, zero degrees/radians.

entries.name: turns::QUARTER_TURN
entries.description: A quarter turn, 90 degrees or π/2 radians.

entries.name: turns::HALF_TURN
entries.description: A half turn, 180 degrees or π radians.

entries.name: turns::THREE_QUARTER_TURN
entries.description: Three quarters of a turn, 270 degrees or 1.5*π radians.

entries.name: sweep::TRAJECTORY
entries.description: Local/relative to the trajectory curve

entries.name: sweep::SKETCH_PLANE
entries.description: Local/relative to a position centered within the plane being sketched on

entries.name: types
entries.description: KCL types. This module contains fundamental types like `number`, `string`, `Solid`, and `Sketch`.

entries.name: gdt
entries.description: Functions for working with geometric dimensioning and tolerancing (GD&T).

entries.name: units
entries.description: Functions for converting numbers to different units.

entries.name: array
entries.description: Functions for manipulating arrays of values.

entries.name: math
entries.description: Functions for mathematical operations and some useful constants.

entries.name: sketch
entries.description: Sketching is the foundational activity for most KCL programs. A sketch is a two-dimensional drawing made from paths or shapes. A sketch is always drawn on a surface (either an abstract plane of a face of a solid). A sketch can be made into a solid by extruding it (or revolving, etc.).

entries.name: solid
entries.description: This module contains functions for modifying solids, e.g., by adding a fillet or chamfer, or removing part of a solid.

entries.name: transform
entries.description: This module contains functions for transforming sketches and solids.

entries.name: turns
entries.description: This module contains a few handy constants for defining turns.

entries.name: hole
entries.description: Definitions of standard holes that could be drilled or cut into solids.

entries.name: std
entries.description: The KCL standard library
